title: Identifying the Limits of Cross-Domain Knowledge Transfer for Pretrained Models

structured.summary: This paper explores the limits of cross-domain knowledge transfer in pretrained language models by testing BERT and LSTM models on tasks with scrambled word input. It finds that BERT shows robust transfer capabilities in classification tasks despite the scrambling, while LSTMs do not perform as well. The study aims to understand the roles of pretraining and fine-tuning in achieving successful cross-domain transfer.

strengths.point: Robustness of BERT in cross-domain transfer
strengths.evidence: The paper demonstrates that BERT significantly outperforms other models in cross-domain transfer, achieving robust transfer rates in classification tasks.

weaknesses.evidence: The paper examines only four classification tasks and two sequence labeling tasks, which might not be representative of the variety of real-world applications for cross-domain transfer.
The related work cites studies like Papadimitriou & Jurafsky (2020), which explore similar cross-domain pretraining, suggesting the methods are not entirely novel.
The focus on scrambling alone may not fully capture cross-domain transfer complexities, as introduced in the explanation of scrambling methods without exploring other data transformations.

questions.question: Why were the specific tasks selected for evaluation?
questions.motivation: Understanding the rationale behind task selection could clarify if these tasks are adequately representative of broader NLP challenges for cross-domain transfer.

critique_points.point_001: Limited evaluation range. The paper examines only four classification tasks and two sequence labeling tasks, which might not be representative of the variety of real-world applications for cross-domain transfer
critique_points.point_002: Potential novelty issues. The related work cites studies like Papadimitriou & Jurafsky (2020), which explore similar cross-domain pretraining, suggesting the methods are not entirely novel
critique_points.point_003: Methodological limitations. The focus on scrambling alone may not fully capture cross-domain transfer complexities, as introduced in the explanation of scrambling methods without exploring other data transformations
